text.title: Gcp Compute Gke Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for the gcp-compute-gke methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale + concrete remediation hook.

rule.statement: GKE clusters MUST enable Workload Identity for all production node-pools.
rule.rationale: Workload Identity replaces node-level SA-key files with short-lived federated tokens — eliminates one of the highest-impact credential-leak vectors.

rule.statement: Teams without dedicated k8s ops capacity MUST default to GKE Autopilot.
rule.rationale: Autopilot offloads node ops + bin-packing to Google; Standard mode requires SRE capacity to operate safely.

rule.statement: Standard-mode clusters MUST split node-pools by workload class (prod / batch / spot).
rule.rationale: Mixed node-pools couple capacity and scheduling decisions; splitting allows per-class taints, autoscaling, and pricing.

rule.statement: Production node-pools MUST be private (`--enable-private-nodes`) with master authorised networks set.
rule.rationale: Public node IPs unnecessarily expose nodes to the internet; private clusters reduce the attack surface.

rule.statement: Production GKE clusters MUST use managed Prometheus + Cloud Logging for observability.
rule.rationale: Managed Prometheus removes the operational burden of running Prometheus while preserving query compatibility.

rule.statement: If the input fails ALL "Applies If" preconditions or matches ANY "Skip If" disqualifier, do not apply this methodology — route the task to the adjacent sibling instead.
rule.rationale: Even flat checklists need an explicit skip leaf in the decision tree; this rule is the universal terminal.
